explanation: This LTL property expresses that the autopilot will eventually transition to the MANEUVER state (indicated by "state_is_MANEUVER") after a failure occurs (indicated by "apfail") while the autopilot is in the STANDBY state (indicated by "state_is_STANDBY"). It utilizes the "until" operator (U) to capture the temporal relationship between the failure event and the state change. The property also implicitly assumes that the autopilot remains in the STANDBY state until the failure occurs.

This LTL property ensures that whenever a failure occurs (apfail), the autopilot will eventually transition from the STANDBY state (state_is_STANDBY) to the MANEUVER state (state_is_MANEUVER). It captures the requirement that the autopilot should change states from STANDBY to MANEUVER upon a failure.

This LTL property ensures that whenever a failure occurs (apfail), the autopilot will eventually transition from the STANDBY state to the MANEUVER state. The property uses the "until" operator (U) to express the condition that the autopilot must remain in STANDBY until the MANEUVER state is reached. This ensures that the autopilot does not enter any other state before transitioning to MANEUVER.

This LTL property ensures that the autopilot will eventually transition from the STANDBY state to the MANEUVER state whenever a failure (apfail) occurs. It does this by stating that whenever the autopilot is in the STANDBY state (state_is_STANDBY) and a failure is detected (apfail), it will eventually enter the MANEUVER state (state_is_MANEUVER).

This property also specifies that the autopilot must remain in the STANDBY state until the failure is detected.  Note that this LTL formula can be simplified, and is shown here for clarity.

This LTL formula captures the requirement that the autopilot should transition from the STANDBY state to the MANEUVER state whenever a failure (apfail) occurs. It uses the atomic propositions "state_is_STANDBY" and "state_is_MANEUVER" to represent the autopilot's states and the atomic proposition "apfail" to represent the occurrence of a failure. The formula states that whenever a failure occurs ("apfail"), eventually the autopilot will be in the MANEUVER state ("state_is_MANEUVER"). This is achieved by using the "until" operator (U) to express that the state will remain in STANDBY ("state_is_STANDBY") until the MANEUVER state ("state_is_MANEUVER") is reached.

This LTL property captures the requirement that whenever the autopilot is in STANDBY mode, it will eventually transition to MANEUVER mode upon detection of a failure. It ensures that the autopilot will react to failures appropriately and move to the MANEUVER state. The property uses the until operator (U) to express this relationship: the autopilot will be in the STANDBY state (state_is_STANDBY) until it is in the MANEUVER state (state_is_MANEUVER) and a failure has occurred (apfail).

This LTL formula captures the requirement that the autopilot should transition from the STANDBY state to the MANEUVER state whenever a failure occurs (apfail). The formula ensures that whenever "apfail" becomes true, the autopilot will eventually transition to the "state_is_MANEUVER" state after being in the "state_is_STANDBY" state.  The formula does not make any assumptions about the state of the autopilot before the failure, it only dictates the behavior after the failure.
